summary_sections.title: Key Concepts: Glaciers & Ice Ages
summary_sections.content_html:
Glaciers are large, persistent bodies of ice that form from compacted snow and move under their own weight.

  • Types: alpine (valley) glaciers and continental ice sheets
  • How glaciers shape the land:
    • Erosion: plucking (pulling rock from bedrock) and abrasion (grinding with embedded rock)
    • Creates U-shaped valleys, cirques, arêtes, horns, fjords
  • Deposition: moraines, drumlins, glacial till, erratics, outwash plains
  • Ice ages: periods when glaciers covered much of Earth's surface. The most recent ended ~11,700 years ago.
  • Milankovitch cycles: variations in Earth's orbit (eccentricity, axial tilt, precession) that trigger glacial/interglacial periods.
